task: Route landing plan-clicks to plan checkout after signup; keep generic Get Started + no-plan dashboard access going to /plans

task: Fix missing OG tags on convattic.com/ landing
q1: Grounded the root cause by building and grepping the three output files before touching code, which correctly redirected the fix away from Landing.tsx to vercel.json
q2: A routing-precedence checklist (filesystem-before-rewrites) would have flagged the shadowed rewrite immediately
q3: Recognizing the symptom (title present, all og missing) as 'raw shell served' is a reusable diagnostic signature for CSR/prerender routing bugs

task: dev-mode identity override (email=dale.cooper, avatar=DC) across frontend
q1: I could have confirmed the env-gate interpretation (MODE vs explicit VITE_ENV) up front, but stating the assumption while proceeding kept momentum without blocking.
q2: A smarter algorithm would flag 'displayed vs functional' uses of the same field (email) during BLAST RADIUS as an explicit checklist, since that distinction drove scope.
q3: A smarter AI would have recognized immediately that a source-level user override cannot yield two-letter 'DC' given the [0] initials logic, avoiding the false-start of centralizing in AuthContext.

task: Add /demo page that auto-runs the HeroDemo on Play click for recording
q1: I'd have anticipated rAF throttling in headless/background tabs before wiring nextFrame() into the driver loop
q2: A smarter algorithm would flag 'requestAnimationFrame in a driver that must run in a possibly-unfocused/recorded tab' as a known premortem gotcha
q3: A fundamentally smarter AI would have recognized the demo already exposes reducer-gated transitions, making a dispatch-driven autoplay obviously superior to DOM-click scraping from the start

task: Make Hero live demo responsive on mobile
q1: Grounded via DOM measurement instead of screenshots (which hung on backdrop-blur), which turned out more rigorous for overflow detection
q2: A smarter algorithm would auto-enumerate interactive states to step through for a stateful component, rather than me hand-driving each reducer transition
q3: Anticipate the flexbox min-width overflow trap up front on any flex-1 input beside a button, rather than discovering it during verification
